title: JSX files are parsed and scanned as plain TypeScript unless they end in .tsx
reasoning: The module recognizes JavaScript/JSX test filenames via the [tj]sx? test-file regex, but JSX parsing is only enabled for .tsx names. Non-test .jsx files therefore go through ts.ScriptKind.TS and LanguageVariant.Standard, so JSX syntax is tokenized/parsed as non-JSX TypeScript. That can skew L and dupMass for JSX sources even though the filename handling implies JS/JSX inputs are in scope.
recommendation: Treat .jsx as JSX in both scriptKind and scanner variant, for example by replacing isTsx with an isJsxLike helper that matches .tsx and .jsx, and selecting ScriptKind.JSX for .jsx.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The characterization matrix includes .ts sources and a .test.ts exclusion case only; it has no .jsx or .tsx source case that would expose JSX parsing/scanning differences.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a measurement case for a .jsx file containing JSX markup and compare it to the legacy oracle or to the same content under a .tsx filename where applicable.
minimumFixScope: Update extension detection in packages/measure/src/main/measure.ts and add a focused JSX measurement test in packages/measure/src/test/measure.test.ts.

history.reasoning: The original evidence files still exist, but the relevant code has changed. packages/measure/src/main/measure.ts now defines isJsxLike at line 30, selects ts.ScriptKind.JSX for .jsx at line 32, and uses isJsxLike to select ts.LanguageVariant.JSX for tokenStream at line 90. packages/measure/src/test/measure.test.ts now includes a focused .jsx regression test at lines 64-68. Typecheck passes. The normal Vitest run could not execute in this read-only sandbox because Vite/Vitest attempted to create temporary files, but executing the current measure.ts source through TypeScript's in-memory transpiler on a .jsx sample returned {"L":30,"complexity":2,"dupMass":0}, confirming the JSX case parses and measures successfully in current code.
commands: pnpm --filter @codenuke/measure exec vitest run --configLoader runner src/test/measure.test.ts

node --input-type=module -e 'import fs from "node:fs"; import ts from "typescript"; const sourcePath = "packages/measure/src/main/measure.ts"; const source = fs.readFileSync(sourcePath, "utf8"); let js = ts.transpileModule(source, { compilerOptions: { module: ts.ModuleKind.ES2022, target: ts.ScriptTarget.ES2022 } }).outputText; js = js.replace("import ts from \"typescript\";\n", "const ts = globalThis.__typescriptForMeasureTest;\n"); globalThis.__typescriptForMeasureTest = ts; const mod = await import(`data:text/javascript;base64,${Buffer.from(js).toString("base64")}`); const result = mod.measure({ "component.jsx": "export const C = ({a, b}) =>
{a < b ? a : b}
;" }); console.log(JSON.stringify(result));'
